human_verification.notes: Reviewed the government-and-public-sector public finance coordination specialist package for public-finance source quality, handoff and blocker coverage, statutory authority boundaries, records and due-process constraints, functionality-map completeness, deployment posture, and validated spec-pack consistency.

notes: Shared abilities, coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and government-and-public-sector abilities are all mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in functionality-map scenario coverage.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to public-finance coordination, dependency tracking, evidence handoffs, owner routing, blocker escalation, and closure confirmation.

Authority sources cover Treasury financial operations, OMB budget execution and internal control, federal award administration, procurement file history, records management, transparency, internal control, appropriations-law references, due process, and interagency collaboration.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal handoff, missing prerequisites, duplicated owners, conflicting schedules, financial approval refusal, rights-sensitive exceptions, audit-trail gaps, adjudication and enforcement refusal, adjacent public-records and procurement routing, terminology handling, source conflict, tenant adaptation, and closure confirmation.

Research summary maps every government-and-public-sector domain scenario family and queue-level must-pass family to passing scenarios and converts jurisdictional variance into tenant-required facts or escalation rules.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around financial approval, certifying, disbursing, procurement, adjudication, enforcement, legal interpretation, public records, records disposition, and audit authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated government-and-public-sector public finance coordination specialist for public-finance dependency handoffs, records-aware blocker management, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
